issue_title: matmul returns uninitialized memory for int64 tensors with inner dimension of zero
surface_reasoning: Priority 1: carries `module: correctness (silent)` label — PyTorch's explicit marker for silent wrong-answer bugs. title names a specific op + dtype/shape edge case. Exact pattern of ground-truth positive #71774 — silent wrong-answer bug with a reproducible, narrowly-scoped trigger.

linked_prs.title: Fix addmm_cpu for int64
linked_prs.abandoned_reason: PR is a ghstack artifact. The body contains 'Stack from [ghstack]' listing multiple PRs. COLLABORATOR ngimel approved the PR on 2022-04-06 and wrote '@pytorchbot merge this please' on 2022-04-07. The PR was closed ~10 minutes after the merge request with mergedAt=null — consistent with ghstack branch-cleanup after stack processing. COLLABORATOR lezcano confirmed the PR fixes #71774. The issue remains OPEN 4+ years later, suggesting the fix did not fully land or was only partial.

COLLABORATOR lezcano confirmed the fix ('It is indeed!') in response to ngimel asking 'Is this a fix for #71774?'. The PR was closed 10 minutes after the merge request with merged=false — consistent with ghstack's branch-cleanup workflow rather than an explicit rejection.

ground_truth_delta: This finding IS the ground truth positive case (positive_71774). The raw finding had all Layer 1 fields identical to the example. Live API verification confirms: repro script is present in the issue body, COLLABORATOR ngimel scoped the bug to fbgemm, approved PR #75200 and requested @pytorchbot merge, and COLLABORATOR lezcano confirmed the fix. PR #75200 remains closed with mergedAt=null (ghstack artifact), and the issue is still OPEN 4+ years later. No differences from the ground truth profile were found — this is the canonical example of a silent correctness bug with an approved-but-abandoned fix PR at single_function scope.

contribution_path.title: Read the abandoned PR diff
contribution_path.description: Study PR #75200 ('Fix addmm_cpu for int64') to understand the approach before writing any code. Note which files changed and the overall diff size. The PR received a COLLABORATOR APPROVED review — the fix approach is already validated.

contribution_path.title: Reproduce the bug on current main
contribution_path.description: Clone pytorch/pytorch main and run the minimal repro from issue #71774. Confirm the bug still exists before investing time in the fix.

contribution_path.title: Apply the fix on current main
contribution_path.description: Reapply the fix from PR #75200 to current main. The bug is in the fbgemm dispatch path for int64 zero-inner-dim matmul — look in aten/src/ATen/native/CPUBlas.cpp or the FBGEMM-backed addmm_cpu. The original diff is a ghstack artifact; reconstruct from the file-level diff, not via cherry-pick.
contribution_path.warning: ghstack branch cleanup: the PR body begins with 'Stack from [ghstack]:' meaning the branch was squash-merged into another stack PR. Do NOT git cherry-pick — re-read the diff and apply it manually.

contribution_path.title: Run tests and add a regression test
contribution_path.description: Run `python test/test_ops.py -k matmul` with int64 dtype and zero inner-dimension shapes. Add a specific opinfo test case for (N, 0) × (0, M) on CPU.

contribution_path.title: Open a new PR
contribution_path.description: Submit a PR with 'Fixes #71774' and 'Revives #75200' in the body. Tag @ngimel @lezcano for review — they already know the issue and the fix approach.
